name: Stone Jar Pickles
recipeInstructions:
Day 1: Boil not quite all of a box of salt in a kettle of water. Pour over approximately a half bushel of small cucumbers and pack into at least a 3-gallon stone jar.
Day 2: Drain the salty water off the cucumbers. Boil a large kettle with 1 box of alum and enough water to fill the kettle. Pour over the cucumbers.
Day 3: Drain off the alum liquid. Boil a kettle of plain water and pour over the pickles.
Day 4: Heat 1 gallon vinegar. Place 1 box of pickling spices in cheesecloth and tie securely. Drop into the vinegar. Pour over the cucumbers and let sit for 9 days.
Day 15: Drain off the liquid from the pickles. Slice the cucumbers. Layer the pickled cucumbers and sugar in a stone crock or glass jars (not necessary to seal). The pickles will keep indefinitely.
